channel.title: Smart Home Hardware Software Development on Home Smart Mesh
channel.description: Recent content in Smart Home Hardware Software Development on Home Smart Mesh

item.title: Thread SensorTag
item.description: Networks / Thread Frameworks / CHIP Overview # nRF52840 on a MS88SF2 module. i²C bus scl P1.13 and sda P1.15 Light sensor VEMLS6030 @0x48 MS8607 with relative humidity @0x40, pressure and temperature @0x76 Reset button P0.

item.title: Switch Cover
item.description: Cover Dumb Switch with Smart Switch # STL Model 01 black and white 3dprints 02 black and white breakout 03 black and white back side

item.title: Web Three Interface
item.description: Webapp Three Interface Github Repo This repo contains webGL Three.js based interface demos for interactions with 3d data using mouse and touch. Final application # This repository has boilerplates samples. A real use case example is a 3d home model. While the focus of this repo is the 3d interactions only, this repo is used in another project linked below where it is connected to real devices and network libraries.

item.title: Datasheets
item.description: ESP32 # Datasheet # Datasheet Blockdiagram # Memory Map # Power Modes # WROOM Datasheet # ESP32-WROOM-32 Datasheet Pin Layout # ESP32-S2 # Datasheet # ESP32-S2 Datasheet Block Diagram # Memory # Power Modes # S2 Technical Reference Manual # TRM

item.title: Light rooms
item.description: Room light east and west # Full screen Open in a new page var button = document.getElementById("btn-bc6c51b9555f0ff6"); button.onclick = ()={ var elem = document.getElementById("ifm-bc6c51b9555f0ff6"); if (elem.requestFullscreen) { elem.requestFullscreen(); } else if (elem.webkitRequestFullscreen) { elem.webkitRequestFullscreen(); } else if (elem.msRequestFullscreen) { elem.msRequestFullscreen(); } };

item.title: Raspi IoT
item.description: Raspi Github Repo Software # Raspbian OS Main services Mosquitto : https://mosquitto.org/ influxDB : https://www.influxdata.com/products/influxdb-overview/ mqtt2influx : py/influx Grafana : https://grafana.com/ graphql sensors : js/graphql x to mqtt Zigbee2mqtt : https://www.zigbee2mqtt.io/ nrf2mqtt : github nRF52_Mesh nrf_mesh miflora2mqtt : https://github.com/RaymondMouthaan/miflora-mqtt-daemon telegram bots2mqtt : js/telegraf based on https://github.com/telegraf/telegraf automation services Light control : py/hue Phantom Load cut : js/pc_control Heat cut : py/heating Home status : py/home_status Roborock button : py/hover user interface 3D webapp : https://github.

item.title: Temperature
item.description: Hot summer # Full screen Open in a new page var button = document.getElementById("btn-388b84c89c2369b2"); button.onclick = ()={ var elem = document.getElementById("ifm-388b84c89c2369b2"); if (elem.requestFullscreen) { elem.requestFullscreen(); } else if (elem.webkitRequestFullscreen) { elem.webkitRequestFullscreen(); } else if (elem.msRequestFullscreen) { elem.msRequestFullscreen(); } }; Xiaomi Aqara vs DIY BME280 # Full screen Open in a new page var button = document.getElementById("btn-f8d70b01c18b3285"); button.onclick = ()={ var elem = document.

item.title: Thread
item.description: Thread SensorTag Zephyr RTOS Project Matter Overview # Full Screen import {setup_svg_panzoom,reset_svg_panzoom} from "/js/setup-svg-pan-zoom.js" function window_reset_position(){ reset_svg_panzoom("openthread-environment-1") } console.log(`into script for "openthread-environment-1"`) window.addEventListener('load', ()={ let embed = document.getElementById("svg-openthread-environment-1") setup_svg_panzoom(embed) }) window.addEventListener('resize', window_reset_position) some elements have link to the corresponding websites Open Thread IPV6 adressing Network discovery test connectivity Cheat sheet # IPv6 bit length : 128 bit = 8x 16 ibts => 00b1:00b2:00b3:00b4:00b5:00b6:00b7:00b8 Link-Local prefix : fe80::/16 Mesh-Local prefix : fd00::/8 Multicast Link-Local : ff02::1 Multicast Mesh-Local : ff03::1 RLOC16 : Routing locator in 16 bits 0x0401 RLOC16 Router mask : 0x0400 IID : Interface Identifier (includes RLOC16) RLOC = Mesh-Local Prefix + IID Specification # request the specification.

item.title: Rover Balance
item.description: Overview # RobLibs Github Organization Open source hobby project, creation of a robot with an original structure that moves in a home environment. The robot structure is a double sided inverted pendulum, with the ability to stand up switching from flat mode (car) to the standing mode (balancing robot), on each of its sides. Further dynamic behaviors to be expected.

item.title: Matter
item.description: Relates to # Zephyr RTOS OpenThread Guide previously known as CHIP Overview # The image above does only focus on the relation between Matter, Thread and Zigbee. Both Matter and Zigbee run on 802.15.4 (optional for Matter), and both are directly or indirectly based on ZCL which is in case of Matter named ZAP Zigbee Cluster Library Advanced Platform.

item.title: UWB DWM1001 dev
item.description: Networks / Ultra Wide Band RTLS Development Board # DWM1001C Module STM32F072 as J-Link OB debugger USB and battery conenctors Raspberry PI Header connector User Leds buttons product page datasheet user manual DWM1001C Module # DW1000 Transciever TWR-RTLS : Two Way Ranging - Real Time Location System nRF52832 for a Bluetooth API LIS2DH12TR STM 3-Axis Motion Detector The product page includes datasheets, schematics, firmware,… product page DW1000 Transciever # IEEE 802.

item.title: Ultra Wide Band
item.description: Microcontrollers / nRF52 / UWB DWM1001 dev An Ultra-Wide-Band networks can form a mesh network, in addition, the main feature is the Real-Time-Locating-System capability. Real Time Locating System # DRTLS : DWM1001 Two-Way-Ranging RTLS RTLS : Real Time Locating System PANS : Positioning and Netowrking Stack eCos : Free Open source Real-Time Operating-System Hardware # UWM Chips available from Decawave, now Qorvo.

item.title: Roborock Valetudo
item.description: Roborock s55 # Valetudo # install Home menu # Map # Telegram app # Roborock chat # the valetudo install section will provide the valetudo server Hypefr server repo this app is using node.js telegraf telegraf dependency the node.

item.title: Home 3D
item.description: {"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"SoftwareApplication","applicationCategory":"HomeAutomation","name":"Smart Home 3D Webapp","offers":{"@type":"Offer","price":"0.00","priceCurrency":"USD"},"operatingSystem":"Windows, OSX, ANDROID"} Webapp 3D Github Repo Getting Started # host this project on your local raspberry pi or see ‘Local host vs Remote host’ section on how to use the live demo create your own glTF model from blender (optional) activate hue bridge connection # adjust the light names to match your hue lights names press the Hue Gateway authorisation button (on the real device) Click on the 3d model of the hue gateway An alert will apear on the screen to wait for confirmation The user creation will proceed and the username will be stored as local storage (ctrl+j in chrome to oben the debug window) activate mqtt connection # adjust the mqtt topic names to match your mqtt devices install a mosquitto version with websocket support Click on the 3d model of the mosquitto gateway Live demo # Full screen Open in a new page var button = document.

item.title: Switch Socket Cover
item.description: Cover Dumb Switch and smart Socket # STL Model Application # More details on the raspberry pi service

item.title: Voronoi
item.description: Webapp # runs with chrome as a web browser Full screen Open in a new page var button = document.getElementById("btn-f7ccb353b1174faa"); button.onclick = ()={ var elem = document.getElementById("ifm-f7ccb353b1174faa"); if (elem.requestFullscreen) { elem.requestFullscreen(); } else if (elem.webkitRequestFullscreen) { elem.webkitRequestFullscreen(); } else if (elem.msRequestFullscreen) { elem.msRequestFullscreen(); } }; Github repo # Voronoi generator webapp Github Repo 3d printing # Voronoi STL Models Description # Parametric Voronoi generator with real time editing and SVG export

item.description: PlatformIO # ESP-Prog Pinout # Connection # Official Guide # Espressif - ESP Prog Guide

item.title: Simple Mesh SensorTag
item.description: Networks / Simple Mesh Frameworks / Raspi IoT Tindie Product Page Concept # It is possible to mount either CR2032 or CR2477 Hardware # board render repo directory nRF52832 BME280 MAX44009 Schematics # Full Screen import {setup_svg_panzoom,reset_svg_panzoom} from "

item.title: Project CHIP
item.description: Moved to # Matter This page will be kept as a transitional link Relates to # Zephyr RTOS Thread Protocol Overview # The image above does only focus on the relation between CHIP, Thread and Zigbee. Both CHIP and Zigbee run on 802.15.4 (optional for CHIP), and both are directly or indirectly based on ZCL which is in case of CHIP named ZAP Zigbee Cluster Library Advanced Platform.

item.title: ESP32 remote
item.description: ESP32 Case for up/down remote control # STL models zip Application # More details on the ESP32 Arduino code 3D printing creation requires sometimes quite a number of iterations

item.title: Software
item.description: Arduino ESP32 # platform package as an idf component ESP-IDF # esp-idf esp-iot-solution mesh-dev-framework mdf prog guide Over The Air Update # >get_idf >idf.py set-target esp32 OTA Github Readme >idf.py menuconfig https://10.0.0.36:8070/hello-world.bin >cp ca_cert.pem ../simple_ota_example/server_certs/ >idf.py -p /dev/ttyUSB0 flash monitor on another linux host, copied ca_cert.pem then tested : >curl -v https://10.0.0.36:8070/simple_ota.bin --cacert ca_cert.pem > simple_ota.

item.title: Simple Mesh
item.description: Simple Mesh Overview # A simpler, more efficient alternative to Z-x, BL-x, Thread, standard RF protocols. Open source SW is provided for custom and off the shelf nRF SoC tags and dongles. Simple Mesh SensorTag Simple Mesh Repo Features # 2.

item.title: nRF52840 USB dongle
item.description: Product Page nRF52840 ARM M4 64 MHz 1024 KB ROM - 256 KB RAM Bluetooth 5, Mesh 802.15.4, Thread, Zigbee USB 2 GetStarted) Applications # custom mesh coordinator nRF52840 Dongle # Pinout # Serial Wire Debug # the usb dongle is referred to as PCA10059 which in projects usually shifts the app to offset 0x1000 as openbootloader usage is expected With serial wire debug, it’s possible to flash applications at same address as the PCA10056 which is at address 0x0000 recovering the openbootloader mode would then require to reflash the MBR with any nRF SDK project make flash_mbr LEDS # LED1 Green : P0.

item.title: Home Assistant
item.description: Official Website Bluetooth # BLE Bluetooth Low Energy # This is a simple integration that tracks BLE devices and beacons without even requiring pairing (privacy notice might be required) BLE Tracker Bluetooth Mesh # One investigation path for integrating Bluetooth mesh in Home Assistant is bridging it through an ip layer using the Bearer Layer of the BLE Architecture BLE Mesh => IP => MQTT-SN => MQTT => Home Assistant

item.title: Handbag hanger
item.description: Handbag hanger # STL Model

item.title: Zigbee
item.description: Devices Overview # Below are examples of used devices in this project As in the above figure, I kept all the light with the hue gateway for a reliable function, and opted for zigbee2mqtt for all other non lighting devices. For a detailed list of used devices and their documentation see the devices list section Frameworks # Philips Hue # ✅ Philips Hue have an open API that can run in a local network, therefore allowing third party apps integration.

item.title: nRF52832 UART dongle
item.description: Board # A usb dongle from the market that includes a 2104 serial to usb converter. Keyword search on shopping websites : nRF52832 USB dongle. Aka “nRF52832-YJ-17017-USB-UART” Software # RF to Serial Gateway # The node connected through serial port will report messages in text format Local Events Upon startup such message is sent nodeid:75;channel:10;reset:1 Listening Upon activation of the uicr config is_listening the node starts acting as a gatewy that will report messages through serial in text mode

item.title: Zephyr RTOS
item.description: Thread Protocol Project Matter Debug with OZone # Ozone : performance analyzer RTOS Awareness : debug an RTOS Steps : make sure to use Ozone version 3.22d or higher in the new project wizard, make sure a peripheral svd file is selected e.

item.title: Under desk shelf
item.description: Under desk shelf # 01 shelf overview 02 shelf top view 03 joint close up 04 joint placement 05 closer view

item.title: micro Python
item.description: nRF52840 usb dongle cross compiler # note CROSS_COMPILE is referencing the system toolchain with loose recommendations 7.3.1/2Q18 or 8.2.1/4Q18. check the toolchain version arm-none-eabi-gcc --version which arm-none-eabi-gcc --version testing with version 9-2020-q2-update - 9.3.1 20200408 build instructions # git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/micropython/micropython.git cd micropython/mpy-cross make cd ../ports/nrf make submodules make BOARD=pca10059 config # usb # each board has the pins configured in a hreader file ports/nrf/boards/pca10059/mpconfigboard.

item.title: House model
item.description: House Model # 01 real house 02 model house 03 model side 04 side zoom 05 side path

item.title: Voronoi Shapes
item.description: Webapp # Voronoi Generator Tool Cells Circle # STL Model Fantasy pen holder # STL Model Fantasy Turtle Flower # STL Model Girafe round # STL Model

item.title: Fisheye Cam OV3660
item.description: 3 Mega Pixels OV3660 Fisheye lens DFOV 120° Ultra low power, sleep current 2μA 270mAh battery PSRAM 8MB Links # Fisheye Timer camera $20 Schematics - ESP32_SUBSYS Schematics - PMS_UART Platformio porting # board definition # local file : boards\m5stack-timer-cam.json should be added to the platformio/platform-espressif32 repo still missing from v2.

item.title: TTGO T-Display
item.description: Simple Example # t-display_controller dependencies # TFT_eSPI_ttgo_t-display fork from Bodmer/TFT_eSPI GfxUi lib_deps = https://github.com/ESP32Home/TFT_eSPI_ttgo_t-display.git#2.3.4_t-display https://github.com/ESP32Home/GfxUi.git#v1.0.1 Example Code # click to expand the example code... #include <Arduino.h>#include <TFT_eSPI.h>#include <SPI.h>#include "GfxUi.h"#include <SPIFFS.h> TFT_eSPI tft = TFT_eSPI(135, 240); GfxUi ui = GfxUi(&tft); void setup() { Serial.begin(115200); tft.init(); tft.setRotation(3); SPIFFS.begin(true);//FORMAT_SPIFFS_IF_FAILED ui.drawBmp("/esp_home.bmp", 0, 0);//'ui' needs tft and SPIFF tft.setCursor(0, 0); tft.

item.title: TTGO T5-EPaper
item.description: Simple example # epaper-TTGO-T5_1_2-hello dependencies # GxEPD lib_ldf_mode = deep+ lib_deps =GxEPD@3.1.0 Example # #include <SPIFFS.h>#include <epaper.h> EPaper epaper; void setup() { Serial.begin(115200); SPIFFS.begin(); epaper.init(); epaper.display.fillScreen(GxEPD_WHITE); epaper.drawBitmap("/esp_home.bmp", 0, 50, true); epaper.displayText("Hello simple", 20, EPaper::RIGHT_ALIGNMENT); epaper.display.update(); } void loop() { } Display # GxGDEW029Z10 2,9 Zoll White / Black / Red 296x128 References # LilyGo epaper ESP-IDF repo LilyGO/ESP32_T5Epaper_2.9inch fork from loboris/ESP32_ePaper_example LilyGo epaper platformio-arduino repo Xinyuan-LilyGO/T5-Ink-Screen-Series fork from TTGO-EPaper-Series Waveshare specification pdf 2.

item.title: M5 Stick Plus
item.description: 6-Axis IMU IR transmitter Microphone RTC LCD (1.14 inch) Lipo Battery Passive Buzzer Product page # m5stack M5StickC Plus $14 Display # 1.14 inch 135x240 Color TFT LCD ST7789v2

item.title: FireBeetle
item.description: Overview # 10 uA deep sleep current USB and external DC battery power inputs (3.3V - 5V) battery charging from USB external DC TP4056 Li-Ion battery charger RT9080 Low Drop out Linear Regulator supported by platformio (board=firebeetle32) PINS # LED : (IO2) VBAT : (A0 - 36 - SENSOR_VP) measure ratio = 0.5 The 0 Ohm resistors R10 and R11 needed to sense the battery voltage by A0-Pin38 are not populated (as of the boards I have).

item.title: M5 Atom Lite
item.description: Overview # Tiny 24 x 24 x 10 mm User Button + Reset RGB Led SK6812 IR Led Pinout # Links # Product Page schematics # Application - Serial to MQTT # The serial to MQTT App creates a request and response topics that will propagate through a second Serial interface Although the board is available on platform-espressif32 it was not avialable on platformio release as of Today, therefore the board is stored locally.

item.title: ESP-Now
item.description: Relates to # ESP-Mesh Wrapper Overview # Github # Github repo ESP-Now reference # docs.espressif esp-now Status # As we can see in the details of the logs below, running an ESP-Now gateway through wifi, results in dropping the reception success rate from 100% to 25%, therefore, a serial ESP-Now gateway would be recommended and not a Wifi one.

item.title: ESP-Mesh
item.description: Relates to # ESP-Now Wrapper Mesh Alternatives # Thread Zigbee Simple Mesh Github repo # Github repo Usage # flash the data file system containing config files config.json and the passwords secret.json with the command >pio run -t uploadfs compile and flash >pio run >pio run -t upload It’s enough repeat for multiple ESP boards and watch the serial output.
